findings.title: SCORE1 regression on Report
findings.detail: SCORE1 is 42.1% worse than baseline. Baseline mean: 71650.0, Current mean: 101840.0. This indicates overall performance degradation at single-thread level.
findings.recommendation: Investigate CPU single-core performance and memory latency on Report.

findings.title: Database test regression on Report
findings.detail: Database test shows 7.2% regression. This suggests storage latency or I/O stack overhead issues.
findings.recommendation: Investigate storage subsystem: disk type (SSD vs HDD), I/O scheduler, virtualization storage driver, and network storage latency.

findings.title: Database test regression on Result DS pu2vwcrisproj6
findings.detail: Database test shows 231.1% regression. This suggests storage latency or I/O stack overhead issues.
findings.recommendation: Investigate storage subsystem: disk type (SSD vs HDD), I/O scheduler, virtualization storage driver, and network storage latency.

findings.title: High variance detected on QMeter 20Runs
findings.detail: 3 metrics show CV > 10.0%. Worst: memory_test_t16_avg_runtime with CV=51.8%. This suggests inconsistent performance, possibly due to background processes, thermal throttling, or resource contention.
findings.recommendation: Ensure test machine is idle during benchmarking. Check for thermal throttling, power management settings, and competing workloads.

findings.title: High variance detected on QMeter 2Runs
findings.detail: 5 metrics show CV > 10.0%. Worst: memory_test_t16_avg_runtime with CV=68.5%. This suggests inconsistent performance, possibly due to background processes, thermal throttling, or resource contention.
findings.recommendation: Ensure test machine is idle during benchmarking. Check for thermal throttling, power management settings, and competing workloads.

findings.title: High variance detected on Report
findings.detail: 5 metrics show CV > 10.0%. Worst: database_test_t16_group_score with CV=35.3%. This suggests inconsistent performance, possibly due to background processes, thermal throttling, or resource contention.
findings.recommendation: Ensure test machine is idle during benchmarking. Check for thermal throttling, power management settings, and competing workloads.

findings.title: High variance detected on Report AzureHBv2
findings.detail: 2 metrics show CV > 10.0%. Worst: database_test_t16_group_score with CV=14.4%. This suggests inconsistent performance, possibly due to background processes, thermal throttling, or resource contention.
findings.recommendation: Ensure test machine is idle during benchmarking. Check for thermal throttling, power management settings, and competing workloads.

findings.title: High variance detected on Result DS pu2vwcrisproj6
findings.detail: 10 metrics show CV > 10.0%. Worst: quill_speed_test_t1_avg_runtime with CV=44.4%. This suggests inconsistent performance, possibly due to background processes, thermal throttling, or resource contention.
findings.recommendation: Ensure test machine is idle during benchmarking. Check for thermal throttling, power management settings, and competing workloads.

findings.title: Mixed results on QMeter 2Runs: algorithm gains offset by other regressions
findings.detail: Algorithm test shows improvement, but Memory tests show regression. The algorithm improvement may not offset the regressions in real-world workloads.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate the relative importance of each test category for your specific Quintiq workload profile.

findings.title: Mixed results on Result DS pu2vwcrisproj6: algorithm gains offset by other regressions
findings.detail: Algorithm test shows improvement, but Quill and Memory tests show regression. The algorithm improvement may not offset the regressions in real-world workloads.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate the relative importance of each test category for your specific Quintiq workload profile.

findings.title: 67 outlier(s) detected on QMeter 20Runs
findings.detail: Found 67 statistical outliers across metrics. These may represent warm-up effects, transient interference, or genuine performance anomalies.
findings.recommendation: Review outlier runs for external factors. Consider excluding warm-up runs from analysis.

findings.title: Infrastructure differs: DCPFOISAPP019 vs poc-quintiq-ES
findings.detail: CPU: Intel(R) Xeon(R) Gold 5220R CPU @ 2.20GHz vs AMD EPYC 7V12 64-Core Processor; Physical cores: 30 vs 64; Memory: 409599MB vs 466893MB; HW type: Unknown vs Cloud environment
findings.recommendation: Account for hardware differences when interpreting performance comparisons.
